titleStmt.title: Letter from John Robert O'Brien to Robert Chalmers, 17 May 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: John Robert O'Brien

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: This is a letter from the secretary of the Congested Districts Board for Ireland, John Robert O'Brien (b. 1856), to Under-Secretary Sir Robert Chalmers (1858–1938). After the Easter rising of 1916 Chalmers was sent to Ireland as under-secretary, replacing Sir Matthew Nathan (1862-1939) who had resigned along with Augustine Birrell (1850-1933), his chief secretary. O'Brien is sending this letter to acknowledge the receipt of a previous letter and asks for a list of address to be sent to him.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary's Office Registered Papers; CSO RP/1916/8409

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
CONGESTED DISTRICTS BOARD FOR IRELAND,
23, Rutland Square, Dublin. 17th, May, 1916 Sir:- I am directed by the Congested District Board for
Ireland to acknowledge the receipt of your letter of the 15th.
instant (No. 7940) with enclosure, and to suggest that the
High Sheriff of County Galway (Mr. James R. Martin, Rockmore,
Athenry) should be asked to give the names and addresses of
the persons referred to in the extract from his letter of the
9th. instant. I am,
Sir,
Your obedient Servant, John O'Brien The Under-Secretary,
Dublin Castle.
